layers.title: Layer 1 — Core Engineering & Systems (The 2013-2030 Bridge)
layers.description: Absolute mastery of the metal, the network, and the browser. No black boxes.

items.title: Systems Design: Database Internals & Core Concepts
items.notes: DB internals (B-Trees vs LSM Trees, indexing strategies, buffer pools), CAP Theorem & PACELC, ACID vs BASE, caching strategies (Redis patterns — cache-aside, write-through, write-behind), CDN architecture & edge caching, DNS resolution & Anycast. Rust implementation: build a B-tree or LSM-based persistent KV store.

items.title: Systems Design: Distributed Systems & Consensus
items.notes: Consensus algorithms (Raft: leader election, log replication, safety), Paxos variants, gossip protocols (SWIM style), consistent hashing & ring topology, quorum reads/writes (NRW), vector clocks, distributed transactions (2PC, 3PC, SAGA), distributed ID generation (Snowflake, ULID). Rust implementation: implement Raft consensus from scratch.

items.title: Systems Design: Scalable Patterns & Infrastructure
items.notes: Load balancing (round-robin, least-connections, IP hash, consistent hashing), rate limiting (token bucket, leaky bucket, sliding window), message queues (Kafka partitioning, RabbitMQ exchanges, Pub/Sub), event sourcing & CQRS, database sharding (hash/range/geo), microservices vs monolith, service mesh sidecars, observability (metrics/traces/logs). Rust implementation: build L4/L7 load balancer and rate limiter.

items.title: Systems Design: Real-World Architectures
items.notes: Twitter (feed fanout, timeline generation, search), YouTube (upload pipeline, CDN streaming), WhatsApp (real-time messaging, E2E encryption, presence), Uber (ride matching, geospatial indexing, ETA), URL shortener, distributed file system (GFS/HDFS), proximity service (quadtrees, geohash, S2), distributed cache (Redis cluster), web crawler (URL frontier, deduplication, politeness), notification system (push/email/SMS). Rust implementation: design prototypes with throughput and latency focus.

items.title: Project Discipline & Execution Habits
items.notes: Practice shipping consistently: scope projects tightly, break work into milestones, write READMEs and issue lists, keep clean commits, document tradeoffs, finish MVPs before adding features, and run retrospectives after each build.

items.title: Communication Skills for Engineers
items.notes: Practice explaining designs, writing concise technical updates, presenting tradeoffs, documenting decisions, asking clear questions, and communicating progress/blockers professionally in interviews, teams, and open-source communities.

items.title: Rust Phase 0: The Syntax Sprint
items.notes: Ownership, Borrowing, Lifetimes (The fundamentals). Learn Strings vs &str, Vecs vs Slices, and Structs/Enums before starting LeetCode.

items.title: Rust Advanced mastery
items.notes: Fearless Concurrency; Send/Sync traits; Arc, Mutex, and RwLock; declarative and procedural macros; Unsafe Rust; FFI with C/C++ including bindgen; Pin and Unpin; async Rust with Future and executor basics; profiling with perf, flamegraph, and cachegrind.

items.title: Python (Advanced) & C++
items.notes: Python: Asyncio, GIL bypass, PyO3 for Rust-Python interop. C++: Performance & DSA heavy lifting, Pointers, Memory Management, SIMD, CMake.

items.title: Polyglot Proficiency: R, Java/Scala, Julia
items.notes: R (Statistical modeling), Java/Scala (Big data + enterprise ML), Julia (High-performance numerical ML)

items.title: JS/TS & V8 Internals
items.notes: Event Loop, Micro/Macro-tasks, JIT Compilation, Hidden Classes, Inline Caching, Garbage Collection including Orinoco/Oilpan GC, ASTs.

items.title: Advanced Web Protocols
items.notes: HTTP/2 & HTTP/3 (QUIC), WebSockets, WebRTC, gRPC, GraphQL, SSE, Webhooks, RSocket.

items.title: Deep Browser APIs
items.notes: Shadow DOM, Service Workers, Web Workers, IndexedDB, WebGPU, WebAssembly (Wasm), Web Bluetooth, WebUSB, File System Access API.

items.title: Auth & Web Security
items.notes: JWT signing algorithms and key rotation, OAuth 2.0 with PKCE flow, OIDC, WebAuthn and Passkeys, CORS, CSRF, XSS, Content Security Policy (CSP), Rate Limiting.

items.title: Infrastructure & Scaling
items.notes: Linux Kernel deep dive (eBPF, namespaces, cgroups, seccomp, overlayfs), Docker, K8s, Terraform, Pulumi, OpenTelemetry.

items.title: DSA (Rust Mode): Arrays & Strings
items.notes: Topics: Two Pointers, Sliding Window. Use Rust iterators (map/fold/filter) for 2030-style performance.

items.title: DSA (Rust Mode): Logic & Sorting
items.notes: Topics: HashMap, Stack, Queue, Binary Search (Quick Sort), Recursion & Backtracking. Bit Manipulation, String Matching (KMP, Rabin-Karp, Z-algorithm).

layers.title: Layer 2 — Mathematics & ML Foundations
layers.description: Understanding the calculus of reality before predicting it.

items.title: Linear Algebra
items.notes: Vectors, Matrices, Eigenvalues/Vectors, SVD, PCA, Tensor operations

items.title: Multivariable Calculus
items.notes: Partial Derivatives, Gradients, Jacobians, Hessians, Chain Rule

items.title: Probability & Statistics
items.notes: Bayes' Theorem, Distributions, Hypothesis Testing, Maximum Likelihood Estimation, Information Theory, Statsmodels

items.title: Classical ML Frameworks
items.notes: Scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost. Regression, Classification, Bias-Variance, Feature Engineering, Model Evaluation, Regularization.

items.title: ML Theory & Alignment
items.notes: Bias-Variance Tradeoff, Regularization (L1/L2), Cross-Validation, Loss Functions

items.title: Data Engineering & Viz
items.notes: NumPy, Pandas, Polars (Rust), Scikit-learn, Ray, Apache Spark, Kafka pipelines. Matplotlib, Seaborn, Plotly, Bokeh, Altair.

items.title: DSA (Rust Mode): Complex Structures
items.notes: Topics: Linked Lists (Box/Option), Trees & Binary Trees (DFS, BFS, Traversals), Heaps, Tries, Hash Tables, Graphs (Dijkstra, Union-Find, DSU with Path Compression), Dynamic Programming (Memoization, Tabulation), Greedy Algorithms. Advanced: Segment Trees, Fenwick Trees, Matrix Exponentiation, FFT.

items.title: Rust for Numerical & Scientific Computing
items.notes: ndarray (N-dimensional arrays, similar to NumPy), nalgebra (linear algebra for ML/graphics), linfa (Rust ML toolkit — equivalent to scikit-learn), statrs (statistical distributions), Peroxide (scientific computing), rust-cv (computer vision). Use Rust for performant numerical pipelines — implement PCA or linear regression from scratch.

items.title: Portfolio Building & Public Proof of Work
items.notes: Turn learning into visible proof: publish polished GitHub repositories, write clear READMEs, add demos/screenshots, explain architecture decisions, document benchmarks, collect certificates, and build a consistent public portfolio.

layers.title: Layer 3 — Deep Learning & Modern AI Architectures
layers.description: Building and training the brains of 2030.

items.title: Neural Network Internals
items.notes: Perceptrons, Activation Functions, Optimizers (Adam), Dropout, Batch Norm, Backpropagation, CNNs, RNNs

items.title: Transformer Architectures
items.notes: Self-Attention, Multi-Head Attention, Positional Encoding, BERT, GPT-n, MoE, Diffusion Models

items.title: DL Frameworks
items.notes: PyTorch (Primary), TensorFlow + Keras, JAX (XLA compilation), HuggingFace Transformers, MindSpore, PaddlePaddle

items.title: Computer Vision
items.notes: OpenCV, YOLO, Detectron2, Segment Anything (SAM)

items.title: NLP & LLMs
items.notes: SpaCy, NLTK, Hugging Face, LangChain, Haystack, LangServe

items.title: Agentic AI & RAG
items.notes: Vector DBs (Pinecone, Qdrant), Advanced RAG, ReAct, Plan-and-Solve, Tool Calling

items.title: Rust Deep Learning Ecosystem
items.notes: Candle (minimalist ML framework by HuggingFace, GPU/CUDA support), Burn (Rust-native DL framework with WGPU backend), tract (ONNX inference engine), ort (ONNX Runtime bindings), safetensors (safe tensor serialization). Use Rust for inference serving — lower latency, smaller footprint than Python.

layers.title: Layer 4 — Web3, Blockchain & Decentralized Systems
layers.description: Engineering trustless, immutable, and uncensorable protocols.

items.title: Ethereum & EVM Deep Dive
items.notes: State Trie, Memory vs Storage, Gas Optimization, Opcode analysis, RPC internals

items.title: Solidity & Smart Contract Security
items.notes: Reentrancy, Front-running, MEV, Auditing tools (Slither, Foundry)

items.title: Layer 2 & Scaling Solutions
items.notes: Optimistic Rollups (Arbitrum), ZK-Rollups (StarkNet, zkSync), Data Availability

items.title: Alternative VMs & Rust Chains
items.notes: Solana Sealevel runtime (BPF, parallel execution), Anchor framework, Polkadot/Substrate FRAME pallets, CosmWasm vs Solidity tradeoffs, MoveVM resource model.

items.title: DApp Architecture
items.notes: Viem, WalletConnect, IPFS/Arweave, The Graph, Account Abstraction (ERC-4337) including bundlers, paymasters, and smart accounts.

items.title: DeFi Primitives
items.notes: AMMs (Uniswap V2/V3), Lending Pools (Aave), Flash Loans, Oracles (Chainlink)

layers.title: Layer 5 — AI x Web3 (The Frontier)
layers.description: Where verifiable compute meets artificial intelligence.

items.title: Zero-Knowledge Machine Learning (ZK-ML)
items.notes: Proving inference on-chain, SNARKs/STARKs for neural networks, EZKL, arkworks (algebraic Rust), bellman, plonky2, halo2.

items.title: Decentralized Compute & Training
items.notes: Gensyn, Akash, Render, Bittensor (Subnets, incentives for intelligence)

items.title: Privacy-Preserving AI
items.notes: TEEs including Intel SGX and AMD SEV, Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE), Multi-Party Computation (MPC), privacy-preserving inference patterns.

items.title: AI Oracles & Agents
items.notes: AI-driven DAO governance, On-chain fraud detection, Autonomous trading agents

layers.title: Layer 6 — Autonomous Agents & High-Performance Runtimes
layers.description: Where autonomous systems meet bare-metal performance.

items.title: Rust-based Runtimes & Wasm
items.notes: Tokio (Async), Rayon (Parallelism), SIMD optimization, Wasm/WASI execution, hyper (HTTP), axum (web framework), tonic (gRPC), tower (middleware).

items.title: Browser Engines & Language Internals
items.notes: V8 Internals, WebKit, Language Engine design, JIT compilation, Garbage Collection algorithms

items.title: Multi-Agent Systems
items.notes: Cognitive architectures (SOAR, ACT-R), Agent Swarms, AutoGPT patterns, inter-agent communication protocols, swarming patterns, coordination and delegation strategies.

items.title: Low-level Optimization
items.notes: CUDA programming, TensorRT, vLLM optimization, Quantization techniques

layers.title: Layer 7 — Cryptographic AI & Post-State Networks
layers.description: Trustless verifiable compute and privacy-preserving intelligence.

items.title: Advanced ZK-AI Pipelines
items.notes: SNARKs/STARKs for verifiable inference, arkworks, snarkVM, bellman, halo2, plonky2, winterfell (STARK prover/verifier), proof recursion.

items.title: Post-State Network Infrastructure
items.notes: FHE for privacy-preserving AI, Decentralized Training Networks, Federated Learning over trustless nodes

items.title: Cloud ML Platforms
items.notes: Google Cloud Vertex AI, AWS SageMaker, Azure ML Studio, Kaggle/Colab, HF Inference Endpoints

suggested_student_path.title: Industry Readiness
items: Master DP, Graphs, Advanced Trees for Interviews
Systems Design: Scalable patterns & real-world architectures

milestones.title: Architecture: High-concurrency Social Feed Schema (PostgreSQL + Redis + Graph)
Web Core: Real-time collaborative canvas (WebSockets + Canvas API)
AI: Full-stack RAG application with custom vector search
Web3: MEV Searcher / Arbitrage Bot (Rust + EVM)
AI+Web3: ZK-ML DApp (Prove you ran a model without revealing inputs)
Capstone: Autonomous On-Chain Agent with Tool Use & Wallet

mlops_devops.title: CI/CD & Infrastructure as Code
mlops_devops.notes: GitHub Actions, Terraform, Ansible, Prometheus, Grafana, Docker/Podman

mlops_devops.title: ML Lifecycle Management
mlops_devops.notes: MLflow, Weights & Biases, Neptune.ai, Model Registries, Feature Stores

mlops_devops.title: High-Performance Serving
mlops_devops.notes: Triton Inference Server, TensorRT, vLLM, quantization, Kubeflow

goals: Systems Design: Distributed Consensus & Raft in Rust
timeline.focus_area: Advanced Rust, statistics, and distributed systems

timeline.focus_area: Deep learning, Web3 basics, and scalable infrastructure patterns

goals: Systems Design: Real-World Architectures (Twitter, YouTube, WhatsApp)
